product.name: ArWoWear T4 Chem Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Vat Dyed Workwear Fabrics Resistant to Industrial Chemicals
product.titleDescriptor: 65% Polyester 35% Cotton, Chemical Resistant Fabric, 200 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 200 g/m², 65% Polyester 35% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Chemical Resistant, 2/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T4 Chem Pro is 200 g/m², vat dyed, 65% polyester 35% cotton merserized workwear fabric.
It is usefull for outdoor summer hard working conditions.
Water and oil repellent (TS EN ISO 4920) function.
It is resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
65% Polyester 35% Cotton, Chemical Resistant Fabric, 200 gr/m'2
200 g/m², 65% Polyester 35% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Chemical Resistant, 2/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>800N; Weft>600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Product lists 'emi' property (EMI shielding relevant to ArWoWear EMK family per taxonomy), but no explicit statement ties it to electrical utilities, arc protection, or high-voltage work.
Chemical resistance and workwear focus could relate to industrial protective clothing, but no explicit welding, foundry or molten-metal splash use is stated.

stage1.noMatchReason: This is a general industrial chemical-resistant workwear fabric (application listed as 'Coveralls') intended for chemical resistance (EN 13034) and outdoor summer working conditions. No explicit sector such as military, firefighting, energy, welding, motorcycle, hi-vis, medical, sport, architecture-building or transport is stated in the product data.

primary.rationale: The product is explicitly described as a workwear fabric for coveralls with chemical resistance (EN 13034), aligning with general industrial protective workwear rather than a narrower named sector like welding or energy, since no explicit metallurgy, electrical, or hazard-specific context is stated.

secondary.rationale: No independent explicit secondary application is supported; EMI property alone does not justify energy sector classification per rule 8, and no welding/foundry context is stated.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: EMI property alone does not establish electrical utility or high-voltage use as required.
